title: Query schema
description: Searchable fields across all harvesters. Domain fields (pdb, pubmed, emdb, biorxiv) are present only on records from that source - use their presence to identify the record type.

identifier.description: The primary persistent identifier for this record (e.g. the DOI minted for it).
The identifier value, typically a DOI

items.description:
An author or contributor.

Not all source APIs provide structured name data, so all name fields are
optional.  At least one of `name` or the `givenName` / `familyName`
pair should be populated by the harvester.

titles.description: One or more titles for this dataset

items.description: A title for the dataset.  Absence of `titleType` indicates the main title.

titleType.description: Controlled vocabulary for title types.  Enumerated as titleType in DataCite Metadata Schema 4.x.

resourceType.description: Resource type classification for this record.

resourceTypeGeneral.description: Resource type controlled vocabulary.  Enumerated as resourceTypeGeneral in DataCite Metadata Schema 4.x.

resourceType.description: Free-text description of the resource type

items.description:
A contributor to the dataset other than the primary creators.

Uses the same name-field structure as Creator, plus a required contributorType.

contributorType.description: Controlled vocabulary for contributor roles.  Enumerated as contributorType in DataCite Metadata Schema 4.x.

items.description: A date associated with the dataset, stored as an ISO 8601 date-time string.

dateType.description: Controlled vocabulary for date types.  Enumerated as dateType in DataCite Metadata Schema 4.x.

language.description: Primary language of the resource (ISO 639-1 or 639-3), e.g. 'en'

alternateIdentifiers.description: Other identifiers for the same resource (e.g. database accession codes)

items.description: An alternative identifier for the same resource (e.g. a database accession or PMID).

alternateIdentifier.description: An identifier for the same resource using a different scheme

alternateIdentifierType.description: The scheme of the alternate identifier, e.g. 'PDB', 'PMID', 'EMDB'

descriptions.description: One or more descriptions of this dataset

descriptionType.description: Controlled vocabulary for description types.  Enumerated as descriptionType in DataCite Metadata Schema 4.x.

items.description:
A subject, keyword, or classification code describing the dataset.

This is a simplified subset of the DataCite subject property — only the
free-text `subject` value is captured.  The full spec also supports
`subjectScheme`, `schemeUri`, and `valueUri` for structured ontology terms,
but most sources provide plain keywords rather than formal ontology entries.
Harvesters should map keyword lists (e.g. PDB `struct_keywords`) to this field,
    or harmonize records using unspecified custom logic.

relatedIdentifiers.description: PID-to-PID relationships. Use when the related resource is fully identified by its persistent identifier and no further description is needed. For citation-style references where title, year, or other metadata should be preserved, use relatedItems instead.

items.description:
A related resource and its relationship to this dataset.

Use when the relationship is expressible as PID-to-PID and no further
description of the related resource is needed (e.g. linking to a related
dataset, software repo, or preprint).  When you need to preserve
bibliographic metadata — title, year, pages — use ``RelatedItem`` instead.

relatedIdentifierType.description: Controlled vocabulary for identifier types.  Enumerated as relatedIdentifierType in DataCite Metadata Schema 4.x.

relationType.description: Controlled vocabulary for relation types.  Enumerated as relationType in DataCite Metadata Schema 4.x.

biorxiv.description: Domain-specific metadata for a bioRxiv or medRxiv preprint.

emdb.description: Domain-specific metadata for an EMDB structure entry.

resolution_angstrom.description: Map resolution in ångströms as reported by the authors

resolution_method.description: Method used to estimate resolution, e.g. 'FSC 0.143 CUT-OFF'

pdb.description: Domain-specific metadata for a PDB structure entry.

method.description: Experimental technique used to determine the structure, e.g. 'X-RAY DIFFRACTION'

resolution_angstrom.description: Diffraction resolution in ångströms; absent for NMR and other non-diffraction methods

polymer_entities.description: Source organisms per polymer entity, preserving the entity-organism association. # TODO: this is a more complex model and domain experts should evaluate full representation

items.description: A polymer entity within a PDB structure, with its source organism.

scientific_name.description: Source organism scientific name from rcsb_entity_source_organism. None when the organism is not recorded (e.g. synthetic constructs).

polymer_type.description: Broad polymer class from entity_poly.rcsb_entity_polymer_type, e.g. 'Protein', 'DNA', 'RNA', 'NA-hybrid', 'Other'.

struct_keywords.description: Raw ``_struct_keywords`` fields from the PDB API, preserved for harmonization.

text.description: Comma-separated keywords supplied by the depositor at submission.

pubmed.description: Bibliographic metadata specific to a PubMed article record.

publication_types.description: NLM publication type labels, e.g. ['Journal Article', 'Review']

NameIdentifier.description: A persistent identifier for a creator, e.g. an ORCID or ISNI.

schemeUri.description: The URI of the identifier scheme, e.g. 'https://orcid.org'
